gomodel:run-figure2/tir1/molecular_funct.snippet: tir-1 annotated to signaling adaptor activity (IDA); TIR-1/SARM1 acts as the upstream scaffold of the intestinal p38 immune cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2/nsy1/molecular_funct.snippet: nsy-1 annotated to MAP kinase kinase kinase activity (IDA); MAPKKK of the p38 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2/sek1/molecular_funct.snippet: sek-1 annotated to MAP kinase kinase activity (IDA); MAPKK of the p38 cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2/pmk1/molecular_funct.snippet: pmk-1 annotated to MAP kinase activity (IDA); the p38 MAPK effector kinase.

gomodel:run-figure2/fshr1/molecular_func.snippet: fshr-1 annotated to G protein-coupled receptor activity (IEA); FSHR-1 is a GPCR acting in intestinal immunity.

gomodel:run-figure2/hsp60/molecular_func.snippet: hsp-60 annotated to ATP-dependent protein folding chaperone (IEA); mitochondrial chaperonin in the UPRmt.

gomodel:run-figure2/atfs1/molecular_func.snippet: atfs-1 annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific (IMP); master UPRmt transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2/hif1/molecular_funct.snippet: hif-1 annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity (IDA); hypoxia/iron-responsive TF regulating antimicrobial genes.

gomodel:run-figure2/aak2/molecular_funct.snippet: aak-2 annotated to AMP-activated protein kinase activity (IDA); AMPK catalytic subunit activated by mtROS in the figure.

gomodel:run-figure2/daf16/molecular_func.justification: daf-16 is the C. elegans FOXO orthologue and a canonical sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor; the annotation slim returned only protein-binding (GO:0005515) rows within the first 15 records, so no TF-activity GO term was directly retrieved. DNA-binding transcription factor activity is the well-established function, not a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2/skn1/molecular_funct.snippet: skn-1 annotated to DNA-binding transcription activator activity, RNA polymerase II-specific (IDA); Nrf2 orthologue driving stress-response genes.

gomodel:run-figure2/hsf1/molecular_funct.snippet: hsf-1 annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity (IMP); heat-shock/stress-response transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2/atf7/molecular_funct.snippet: atf-7 annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity (ISS); bZIP TF, the terminal transcriptional output of PMK-1/p38 immune signaling.

gomodel:run-figure2/zip4/molecular_funct.snippet: zip-4 annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific (IMP); bZIP factor activating the ESRE program.

gomodel:run-figure2/cebp1/molecular_func.snippet: cebp-1 annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity (IEA); C/EBP bZIP factor contributing to ESRE activation.

gomodel:run-figure2/cebp2/molecular_func.snippet: cebp-2 annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity (IEA); C/EBP bZIP factor contributing to ESRE activation.

gomodel:run-figure2/zip2/molecular_funct.snippet: zip-2 annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity (IEA); bZIP factor activating ESRE and antimicrobial genes upon translational inhibition.

gomodel:run-figure2/nhr86/molecular_func.snippet: nhr-86 (HNF-4-like nuclear receptor) annotated to DNA-binding transcription factor activity (IEA); drives antimicrobial genes in response to pyocyanin.

gomodel:run-figure2/tir1/part_of.justification: tir-1/SARM1 is the apical scaffold initiating the intestinal p38 (PMK-1) innate immune response in this figure. The retrieved slim listed only signal transduction (GO:0007165, a gocheck_do_not_annotate-prone parent) and developmental terms, so I selected the more specific, contextually correct innate immune response rather than a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2/nsy1/part_of.snippet: nsy-1 annotated to MAPK cascade (GO:0000165, IGI); the MAPKKK step of the p38 immune cascade. (BP placed as MAPK cascade.)
nsy-1 annotated to MAPK cascade (GO:0000165, IGI); the MAPKKK step of the p38 immune cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2/sek1/part_of.snippet: sek-1 annotated to MAPK cascade (GO:0000165, IGI); the MAPKK step of the p38 immune cascade.

gomodel:run-figure2/pmk1/part_of.justification: pmk-1 is the terminal p38 MAPK of the intestinal innate immune cascade in this figure. The slim returned MF terms (MAP kinase activity) but no immune BP within the first 15 rows; innate immune response is the established, contextually correct process for PMK-1, not merely a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2/fshr1/part_of.snippet: fshr-1 annotated to defense response to Gram-negative bacterium (IMP); intestinal GPCR contributing to antibacterial defense.

gomodel:run-figure2/hsp60/part_of.snippet: hsp-60 annotated to mitochondrial unfolded protein response (IMP); chaperone marker/effector of the UPRmt branch.

gomodel:run-figure2/atfs1/part_of.snippet: atfs-1 annotated to mitochondrial unfolded protein response (IMP); master UPRmt transcription factor downstream of mitochondrial stress.

gomodel:run-figure2/hif1/part_of.snippet: hif-1 annotated to regulation of DNA-templated transcription (IEA); iron/hypoxia-responsive TF regulating antimicrobial defense gene expression.

gomodel:run-figure2/aak2/part_of.justification: The figure shows AMPK (aak-2) being activated by mitochondrial ROS and relaying to nuclear gene expression. aak-2's slim had AMPK MF + metabolic/lifespan BP terms but no ROS-response BP in the first 15 rows; cellular response to reactive oxygen species best captures the figure's mtROS-activation context. This is a reasoned inference, not a raw figure read of a drawn arrow.

gomodel:run-figure2/daf16/part_of.justification: daf-16/FOXO acts as a transcription factor regulating cellular stress-response genes in the figure. The retrieved slim returned only protein-binding rows, so regulation of DNA-templated transcription is assigned from DAF-16's established TF role rather than from a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2/skn1/part_of.justification: skn-1/Nrf2 regulates cellular stress-response genes (RNA Pol II transcription) in the figure. Its slim returned DNA-binding/activator MF terms but no regulation-of-transcription BP in the first 15 rows; the BP is inferred from SKN-1's established transcription-activator role, consistent with the IDA activator MF, not a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2/hsf1/part_of.justification: hsf-1 regulates cellular stress-response genes via RNA Pol II transcription in the figure. Its slim listed DNA-binding MF and chromatin-binding terms but no regulation-of-transcription BP in the first 15 rows; the BP is inferred from HSF-1's established transcription-factor role, not a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2/atf7/part_of.snippet: atf-7 annotated to defense response to Gram-negative bacterium (IMP); terminal TF driving antimicrobial defense genes downstream of PMK-1.

gomodel:run-figure2/zip4/part_of.snippet: zip-4 annotated to regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II (IMP); activates the nuclear ESRE program.

gomodel:run-figure2/cebp1/part_of.justification: cebp-1 contributes to ESRE activation (transcriptional regulation) in the figure. Its slim returned only GO:0003700 + protein-binding rows, so regulation of DNA-templated transcription is inferred from its bZIP transcription-factor role, not a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2/cebp2/part_of.snippet: cebp-2 annotated to antibacterial innate immune response (IMP) and defense response to Gram-negative bacterium; contributes to ESRE/antimicrobial gene activation.

gomodel:run-figure2/zip2/part_of.snippet: zip-2 annotated to regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II (IBA); bZIP TF activating ESRE and antimicrobial genes after translational inhibition.

gomodel:run-figure2/nhr86/part_of.snippet: nhr-86 annotated to regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II (IBA); HNF-4-like nuclear receptor driving antimicrobial genes in response to pyocyanin.

gomodel:run-figure2/tir1/occurs_in.snippet: TIR-1 drawn as a membrane-protein glyph on the left side of the intestinal-cell membrane; no plasma-membrane GO annotation was present in the retrieved slim, so localization is taken from the figure.

gomodel:run-figure2/fshr1/occurs_in.snippet: fshr-1 annotated to plasma membrane (IEA/IBA); GPCR at the intestinal cell membrane, consistent with the figure's upper-right membrane receptor glyph.

gomodel:run-figure2/hsp60/occurs_in.snippet: hsp-60 annotated to mitochondrion (IDA); consistent with the figure placing HSP-60 next to the mitochondria.

gomodel:run-figure2/atfs1/occurs_in.snippet: atfs-1 annotated to nucleus (IDA); UPRmt transcription factor that translocates to the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure2/cebp2/occurs_in.snippet: cebp-2 annotated to nucleus (IEA); nuclear bZIP transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2/zip2/occurs_in.snippet: zip-2 annotated to nucleus (IDA); nuclear bZIP transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2/nhr86/occurs_in.snippet: nhr-86 annotated to nucleus (IDA); the figure explicitly places NHR-86 inside the nucleus compartment.

gomodel:run-figure2/hif1/occurs_in.justification: hif-1 is a nuclear bHLH-PAS transcription factor; the retrieved slim did not include a nucleus CC row, so the nuclear localization is assigned from its established TF role, consistent with the figure placing it among the nuclear factors.

gomodel:run-figure2/daf16/occurs_in.justification: daf-16/FOXO acts as a nuclear transcription factor; the slim returned only protein-binding rows, so nuclear localization is assigned from its established TF role rather than a figure read.

gomodel:run-figure2/skn1/occurs_in.justification: skn-1/Nrf2 is a nuclear transcription factor; the slim listed DNA-binding/activator MF terms but no nucleus CC, so nuclear localization is assigned from its established TF role.

gomodel:run-figure2/hsf1/occurs_in.justification: hsf-1 is a nuclear heat-shock transcription factor; the slim included chromatin/DNA-binding terms but no nucleus CC, so nuclear localization is assigned from its established TF role.

gomodel:run-figure2/atf7/occurs_in.justification: atf-7 is a nuclear bZIP transcription factor; the slim returned DNA-binding and MAP-kinase-binding terms but no nucleus CC, so nuclear localization is assigned from its established TF role.

gomodel:run-figure2/zip4/occurs_in.justification: zip-4 is a nuclear bZIP transcription factor acting on the nuclear ESRE program; the slim lacked a nucleus CC row, so nuclear localization is assigned from its established TF role.

gomodel:run-figure2/cebp1/occurs_in.justification: cebp-1 is a nuclear C/EBP bZIP transcription factor; the slim returned only GO:0003700 + protein-binding rows, so nuclear localization is assigned from its established TF role.

gomodel:run-figure2/tir1/causal/gomodel:.snippet: Peterson et al. 2022 (eLife): pathogen infection and cholesterol deficiency activate the C. elegans p38 immune pathway through a TIR-1/SARM1 phase transition, with TIR-1 acting upstream to activate the NSY-1 MAPKKK. Edge: TIR-1 signaling-adaptor activity directly positively regulates NSY-1 MAP kinase kinase kinase activity.

gomodel:run-figure2/nsy1/causal/gomodel:.snippet: Reactome 'MAPK family signaling cascades' (C. elegans): the MAPKKK directly phosphorylates and activates the MAPKK. Edge: NSY-1 MAPKKK activity directly positively regulates SEK-1 MAPKK activity. NSY-1 also carries a curated mitogen-activated protein kinase kinase binding annotation (GO:0031434).
Peterson et al. 2022 (eLife): TIR-1/SARM1 phase transition activates the C. elegans p38 immune cassette NSY-1->SEK-1->PMK-1; corroborates NSY-1 MAPKKK directly positively regulating SEK-1 MAPKK.

gomodel:run-figure2/sek1/causal/gomodel:.snippet: Reactome 'MAPK family signaling cascades' (C. elegans): the MAPKK directly phosphorylates and activates the p38 MAPK. Edge: SEK-1 MAPKK activity directly positively regulates PMK-1 MAP kinase activity. SEK-1 carries a curated MAPKKK-binding annotation (GO:0031435).
Peterson et al. 2022 (eLife): the C. elegans p38 immune cassette signals NSY-1->SEK-1->PMK-1; corroborates SEK-1 MAPKK directly positively regulating PMK-1 p38 MAPK.

gomodel:run-figure2/pmk1/causal/gomodel:.snippet: Raj et al. 2023 (Nat Commun): activation of the p38/ATF-7 signaling pathway; PMK-1 (p38) directly phosphorylates the bZIP factor ATF-7, switching it from repressor to activator of defense genes. ATF-7 carries a curated MAP kinase binding annotation (GO:0051019). Edge: PMK-1 MAP kinase activity directly positively regulates ATF-7 transcription factor activity.

Figure draws DAF-16 (FOXO) in the transcription-factor stack as a target of PMK-1 that then regulates cellular stress-response genes. No primary paper establishing a direct PMK-1->DAF-16 step was found in the literature search (the canonical PMK-1 TF target is SKN-1/ATF-7), so this drawn relationship is recorded at the figure tier with an unspecified-mechanism positive predicate (causally upstream of, positive effect) for the curator to verify.
